source_inspiration.name: nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill
source_inspiration.used_for: Design reasoning structure, style taxonomy, color/typography/UX checklist inspiration.

source_inspiration.name: creativetimofficial/ui
source_inspiration.used_for: Component and block catalog mapping inspiration for shadcn/Tailwind prototypes.

selection_principles: Product task and information density override visual novelty.
Operational tools should default to restrained, scannable, low-drama styles.
Expressive styles are allowed when they improve brand signal, editorial impact, or product memorability.
Every selected style must include avoid_for and quality_gates before prototype generation.
A user-explicit style request can override default suitability, but the output must keep risk notes.

styles.name: Swiss Utility
styles.description: Restrained grid-first interface style for products that must be scanned, compared, and used repeatedly.
avoid_for: brand landing pages needing strong emotional recall

tokens.typography: Inter or system sans; tabular numbers for metrics
tokens.layout: 12-column grid, tight section hierarchy, strong table and form rhythm

quality_gates: Can a repeated user complete the top task without reading decorative copy?
Do tables, filters, and actions stay aligned at desktop and laptop widths?
Are accent colors reserved for state and action instead of decoration?

styles.name: Refined SaaS
styles.description: Quiet, polished SaaS style with stronger brand signal than pure utility while keeping work surfaces usable.
avoid_for: high-risk financial operations where decorative visuals reduce trust
mobile-first consumer apps needing playful identity

tokens.typography: Inter, Geist, or Manrope; medium weights for section labels
tokens.layout: dashboard shell with clear primary rail and focused content panes

quality_gates: Is the UI recognizably product software rather than a marketing page?
Are shadows and gradients restrained enough for daily work?
Does every card carry distinct information rather than decoration?

styles.name: Concrete Editorial
styles.description: Raw concrete, stone-gray surfaces, architectural spacing, strong type contrast, and restrained accent color.

tokens.typography: Neue Haas Grotesk, Satoshi, Inter, or a strong grotesk; optional mono captions
tokens.texture: subtle concrete grain, matte panels, hairline rules
tokens.layout: asymmetric editorial grid with large type, strong vertical rhythm, and sparse modules
tokens.motion: slow reveals or none; avoid playful bounce

quality_gates: Does the concrete texture stay subtle enough to preserve text contrast?
Is the style supporting product trust instead of making the UI look unfinished?
Are dense data tables avoided or converted to clean utility surfaces?

styles.name: Brutalist Product
styles.description: Hard edges, high contrast, visible structure, oversized type, and intentionally direct composition.

tokens.layout: blocky grids, exposed containers, strong outlines

quality_gates: Does the style remain usable after the first impression?
Are thick borders used to clarify hierarchy rather than create clutter?
Is contrast accessible in all states?

styles.name: Glass Depth
styles.description: Layered translucent surfaces with depth, blur, and luminous accents.

tokens.typography: Inter, Geist, or SF Pro; light-to-medium contrast
tokens.texture: backdrop blur, transparent panels, subtle glow
tokens.layout: layered panels over real product or generated scene backgrounds
tokens.motion: depth parallax only when it does not distract

quality_gates: Is text readable without relying on blur or glow?
Are there fewer than three overlapping glass layers per viewport?
Does the UI still work when transparency is reduced?

styles.name: AI Lab Dark
styles.description: Technical dark interface for model routing, evaluation, observability, and AI operations.

tokens.typography: Geist, Inter, IBM Plex Sans; mono for code and tokens
tokens.layout: console shell, split panes, run timeline, evidence cards
tokens.motion: stateful status transitions, no decorative animation

quality_gates: Can users compare model outputs side by side?
Are warnings and failures visually stronger than decorative accents?
Do code and data regions use stable dimensions?

styles.name: Editorial Magazine
styles.description: Content-led layout with strong headlines, generous whitespace, image rhythm, and readable long-form sections.

tokens.typography: Serif or editorial display paired with clean sans
tokens.layout: article grid, pull quotes, image-led sections

quality_gates: Is the reading line length controlled on desktop?
Does the H1 carry the product or topic directly?
Are images specific and inspectable, not atmospheric filler?

styles.name: Bauhaus Grid
styles.description: Geometric composition, primary accents, strict grid, and functional modernist typography.

tokens.layout: visible modular grid with occasional geometric anchors

quality_gates: Do geometric accents avoid hiding real product content?
Is the color palette limited to action and navigation cues?
Can compact UI elements still fit text cleanly?

styles.name: Minimal Luxury
styles.description: Quiet premium visual language with high whitespace, restrained contrast, and precise typography.

tokens.typography: Elegant serif or refined grotesk; avoid excessive weights
tokens.layout: large image, precise copy, sparse controls

quality_gates: Is the product or place visible in the first viewport?
Does whitespace support inspection rather than hide missing content?
Are CTAs clear enough despite minimal styling?

styles.name: Warm Humanist
styles.description: Approachable product UI for coaching, learning, wellness, and assistant experiences.

tokens.typography: Rounded or humanist sans; readable body text
tokens.layout: guided steps, progress panels, reassuring empty states

quality_gates: Does the tone stay mature rather than childish?
Are reminders and nudges controllable by the user?
Do warm colors avoid reducing contrast?

styles.name: Retro Terminal
styles.description: Command-line inspired product language with mono type, stark panels, and system-like interaction.

tokens.typography: IBM Plex Mono, JetBrains Mono, or Geist Mono
tokens.layout: terminal panes, command prompts, logs, keyboard-first affordances

quality_gates: Does mono type remain readable for non-code content?
Are primary actions discoverable without knowing commands?
Is the retro treatment a product fit rather than decoration?

styles.name: Neo Clay
styles.description: Soft 3D surfaces, rounded geometry, tactile cards, and playful depth.

tokens.layout: large touch targets, simple panels, friendly progression

quality_gates: Does the style match the user's age and task seriousness?
Are shadows consistent and not muddy?
Can text fit inside large rounded components on mobile?

styles.name: Data Newsroom
styles.description: Analytical editorial style for explaining metrics, market movement, and research findings.

tokens.typography: Readable sans for UI plus optional serif headlines
tokens.layout: headline insight, chart grid, evidence notes, source trace
tokens.motion: none unless chart interaction requires it

quality_gates: Does every chart state its question and decision implication?
Are sources and confidence visible?
Can users distinguish facts from model inferences?
